mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL reduces real equity valuations through inflation discount and anticipated policy response, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that impairs consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline of retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) identifies a broad demand-supply contraction that amplifies downside earnings risk beyond either series alone, causing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO month-over-month changes yields a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, additional month-over-month increases tighten financial conditions abruptly, causing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns through rapid discount rate repricing.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and rises further, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals rising operating leverage and capacity utilization that translate into higher corporate profitability, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% from its 3-month prior level forecasts collapsing construction multipliers and durable goods demand, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 10% over 3 months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous month-over-month increase in both unemployment (UNRATE) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) generates a stagflationary profit margin squeeze that drives negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, an effect neither variable produces independently.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in UNRATE and CPIAUCSL do not produce significantly more negative 20-day returns than either series alone (interaction t-stat < 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: Following a quarter of negative real GDP growth (GDPC1), a month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals demand normalization that lifts earnings expectations, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In quarters following negative GDPC1 growth, month-over-month RSAFS increases are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.
